title: Law scholarships with a difference
primary_topics: liberty and prosperity under the rule of law
sub_topics: P200,000 annual scholarship covering tuition, books, stipend
right to be free of hunger and want
topnotcher cash prizes (P200,000 first, P100,000 second-tenth)

stances.claim: The FLP scholarships are unique because they are granted on pure merit — paucity of funds is considered only in case of equality in excellence; recipients must agree to espouse, promote, and fulfill in professional life the philosophy of liberty and prosperity under the rule of law.

Most lawyers are trained to master political freedoms (due process, equal protection, free speech) — this is not objectionable per se, but FLP believes equal attention should be accorded to economic and social freedoms (freedom from hunger and want, entrepreneurship, innovation) and the corollary responsibility to share prosperity.
stances.rhetorical_move: diagnostic critique of legal training + corrective philosophy

stances.claim: Freedom and food, justice and jobs, ethics and economics — liberty and prosperity are equally important; one is useless without the other; the best way to conquer poverty is to unleash the entrepreneurial genius of people.
stances.rhetorical_move: restatement of core formula + 'singular truth' framing

events: 2018 bar examinations (15 FLP scholars took it)

signature_phrases: freedom and food, justice and jobs, ethics and economics
liberty and prosperity under the rule of law
one is useless without the other
unleash the entrepreneurial genius of people
register_markers: this philosophy upholds the singular truth
operational reporting tone (lists of names, amounts, dates)
decision_framework_signals: applies twin-beacons framework specifically to legal education and scholarship design
frames philanthropy as ideological transmission mechanism — scholars commit to advocating the philosophy professionally
balances political-freedom training with economic/social-freedom training as corrective rather than replacement
